sourceFull: Phandelver and Below: The Shattered Obelisk

name: Clockwork Bronze Scout
sourceFull: Mordenkainen Presents: Monsters of the Multiverse
languages: understands one language of its creator but can't speak
actions: Lightning Flare (Recharges after a Short or Long Rest)

name: Clockwork Defender
lore: A clockwork defender is a mechanical quadruped that vaguely resembles a hound. Its eyes glow and can project intense but harmless beams of light.
languages: understands the languages of its creator but can't speak

name: Clockwork Dragon
lore: These intricately crafted constructs are typically made to reflect the forms of the metallic dragons. Plated in brass, bronze, copper, or faux gold and silver, they are often taken for fine draconic statues at first glance.

name: Clockwork Horror
lore: Clockwork horrors are ruthless insectile automatons—eldritch machines encased in adamantine or some other precious metal. They roam Wildspace in commandeered spelljamming ships, gathering the precious metals and crystals they need to build more of their kind.

name: Clockwork Iron Cobra
sourceFull: Mordenkainen Presents: Monsters of the Multiverse
languages: understands one language of its creator but can't speak

name: Clockwork Kraken
languages: understands the languages of its creator but can't speak

name: Clockwork Oaken Bolter
sourceFull: Mordenkainen Presents: Monsters of the Multiverse
languages: understands one language of its creator but can't speak

name: Clockwork Observer
lore: A clockwork observer serves as an aerial spy for its creator. It looks like a mechanical, grapefruit-sized orb suspended under softly humming propeller blades.
languages: understands the languages of its creator but can't speak

name: Clockwork Stone Defender
sourceFull: Mordenkainen Presents: Monsters of the Multiverse
languages: understands one language of its creator but can't speak

name: Cloud Giant Destiny Gambler
sourceFull: Bigby Presents: Glory of the Giants
lore: Cloud giants rise in the ordning by amassing valuable and beautiful treasures—often by gambling. While many cloud giants are content with risking their wealth in games with each other, those who aim for the top of their ordning challenge all manner of beings in games of chance and wit.

name: Cloud Giant of Evil Air
sourceFull: Bigby Presents: Glory of the Giants
lore: Given their inherent link to elemental air, cloud giants who turn from the gods of the Ordning often gravitate to the service of Yan-C-Bin, Prince of Evil Air. These giants' cunning, charisma, and sheer physical and magical might make them well suited to leadership positions in the cults of Elemental Evil.

name: Cloud Giant Smiling One
sourceFull: Mordenkainen Presents: Monsters of the Multiverse
lore: Smiling ones are cloud giants who honor and emulate the craftiness and deceit of the deity Memnor above all else. They are tricksters supreme who use sleight of hand, deception, misdirection, and magic in their pursuit of wealth.

name: Cockatrice
lore: The cockatrice looks like a hideous hybrid of lizard, bird, and bat, and it is infamous for its ability to turn flesh to stone. These omnivores have a diet that consists of berries, nuts, flowers, and small animals such as insects, mice, and frogs-things they can swallow whole.

name: Cogwork Archivist
lore: Programmed with knowledge of Strixhaven's extensive lore catalog, cogwork archivists serve as keepers of the university's various libraries. The archivists' towering metal frames are equipped with long, articulated limbs and retractable conservator tools, which they use to organize and preserve documents from throughout Strixhaven's winding history.

name: Coldlight Walker
sourceFull: Icewind Dale: Rime of the Frostmaiden
lore: Some humanoids who died from extreme cold but whose spirits languish in the mortal world become coldlight walkers, burning with frigid fury at the meaninglessness of life. Their frostbitten corpses emit a spectral light so intense that mortal eyes can barely stand to look at them.

name: Colossus
lore: {@i Titanic Vessel of Divine Might} Colossi are massive Constructs created by the devout to reflect the nature of a deity, which could be benevolent or wicked. Colossi thrum with incredible magic and work divine will on the land.
languages: understands Celestial and Common but can't speak

name: Colossus of Akros
lore: An enormous golem of bronze and iron overlooks the path leading to the polis of Akros. Though it is rarely called on to defend the polis, the sight of its towering form is enough to ease the minds of the populace.
languages: understands Common and Celestial but can't speak

name: Combat Robot
lore: Combat robots are designed for security or military action. They make able sentries and are usually programmed to take intruders prisoner before resorting to lethal force.
languages: Common plus the languages spoken by its creator

name: Commoner
lore: Commoners include peasants, serfs, slaves, servants, pilgrims, merchants, artisans, and hermits.

name: Conclave Dryad
lore: The lush forests that once grew on Ravnica are gone, but the dryads remain, striving to bring the sprawling city and the verdant green of nature into harmony. Dryads believe that their efforts are the will of Mat'Selesnya, the soul of the world, and they spread their teachings through every Selesnya enclave.

name: Conjurer
lore: Conjurers are specialist wizards who summon creatures from other planes and create materials out of thin air. Some conjurers use their magic to bolster armies or destroy enemies on battlefields, while others use summoned creatures to guard their lairs.
traits: Benign Transportation (Recharges after the Conjurer Casts a Conjuration Spell of 1st Level or Higher)

name: Conjurer Wizard
sourceFull: Mordenkainen Presents: Monsters of the Multiverse

name: Constructed Commoner
lore: To populate the demiplane without endangering any innocents, the Harpers created constructs out of wooden mannequins to act as villagers. Magic makes them look like flesh-and-blood people, and the constructs conduct themselves as convincingly real humanoids who are simply living their lives in Wisteria Vale.

name: Copper Greatwyrm
traits: Metallic Awakening (Recharges after a Short or Long Rest)

name: Coral
lore: {@note Created by Kenzie C.} Coral lives in the ocean and in the clouds.

name: Core Spawn Crawler
lore: The smallest and most numerous of the core spawn, these eyeless creatures scurry through the subterranean darkness with the help of their four irregular, gangly arms and hooked prehensile tails. Core spawn crawlers rarely travel alone, and a group of these agile predators is known as a vein of crawlers.
languages: understands Deep Speech but can't speak

name: Core Spawn Emissary
lore: These airborne predators serve as assassins and sentinels for the core spawn. The terrible thrum of its insectoid wings and a chittering of mandibles announces an emissary's arrival.
languages: understands Deep Speech but can't speak

name: Core Spawn Seer
lore: Core spawn seers are humanoid arcanists corrupted by the eldritch power of the Elder Evils through blasphemous rites or accursed encounters. Ravaged by otherworldly radiation and disease, their bodies are covered in horrible protrusions of fluorescent crystals, which emit a psychedelic glow from beneath the tattered folds of their robes.

name: Core Spawn Worm
lore: This invertebrate horror has quivering, barbed tentacles set around its massive, toothy maw. The worm's cracked and stony hide pulses with a dull orange glow, as if it might be composed of primordial lava perpetually on the verge of hardening into solid rock.
languages: understands Deep Speech but can't speak

name: Cornelius Watson
lore: Doc Watson (only his mother calls him Cornelius) is the consummate academic. He has an eclectic collection of interests and rarely stays focused on a single subject for longer than a few months.

name: Corpse Flower
sourceFull: Mordenkainen Presents: Monsters of the Multiverse
lore: A corpse flower can sprout atop the grave of an evil necromancer or the remains of powerful Undead creatures. Unless it is uprooted and burned while it is still a seedling, the corpse flower grows to enormous size over several weeks, then tears itself free of the earth and begins scavenging Humanoid corpses from battlefields and graveyards.

name: Corrupted Avatar of Lurue
lore: In normal circumstances, the avatar of Lurue is a breathtaking sight; her silver, stardust-like mane, her shimmering hooves, and her perfectly formed spiral horn elicit awe from even the most hardened souls. As powerful and striking as she is in her true form, she's just as wicked in her alternative state.

name: Corrupted Giant Shark
lore: In addition to its vicious bite, this giant shark projects a psychic aura that causes painful mental distress in other creatures nearby. The crystals that corrupt the shark's body enable it to regenerate, and only radiant energy or particularly devastating attacks can overcome this unnatural defense.

name: Corrupted Rat
lore: Corrupted by a malevolent ex-adventurer's magic, the rats of Elmshire are mottled and sickly. Their organs glow and bubble beneath their scabby hides as if ready to pop.

name: Corruption Devil (Paeliryon)
lore: One of the stronger greater devils, the paeliryon focus on corruption and obtaining mortal souls. While they can be found on almost every layer of the Nine Hells, paeliryons are particularly prominent in Dis and Malbolge, where they hold positions of power.

name: Cosmic Horror
lore: Cosmic horrors are colossal, malevolent entities that occasionally slip the bonds of the Far Realm and find themselves adrift on the Astral Plane. Drawn to the light of distant stars, these creatures invade Wildspace systems and lay waste to worlds.

name: Couatl
lore: Couatls are benevolent serpentine beings of great intellect and insight. Their brilliantly colored wings and gentle manner speak to their celestial origins.

name: Cow
lore: There are many kinds of cattle, from common oxen to more unusual, magical variants.

name: Crab Folk
lore: The crab folk are the result of a magical ritual used long ago by a mighty green hag to transform her ogre minions into more useful servants. All crab folk that now exist are descended from those original creations, with most living in small, isolated clutches.

name: Cradle of the Cloud Scion
sourceFull: Bigby Presents: Glory of the Giants

name: Cradle of the Fire Scion
sourceFull: Bigby Presents: Glory of the Giants

name: Cradle of the Frost Scion
sourceFull: Bigby Presents: Glory of the Giants

name: Cradle of the Hill Scion
sourceFull: Bigby Presents: Glory of the Giants

name: Cradle of the Stone Scion
sourceFull: Bigby Presents: Glory of the Giants

name: Cradle of the Storm Scion
sourceFull: Bigby Presents: Glory of the Giants

name: Crag Cat
lore: The creature referred to in Northlander lore as the Hunter of Men is a sure-footed predator that can be found anywhere except deep forest, preferring ledges and cliffs in the mountains. Its cry resembles a human scream of terror.

name: Cranium Rat
sourceFull: Mordenkainen Presents: Monsters of the Multiverse
lore: Mind flayers create cranium rats by bombarding rats with psionic energy. Cranium rats are somewhat smarter than ordinary rats and behave as such.

name: Cranium Rat Squeaker
lore: The cranium rats squeakers of Sigil have no connection to the mind flayers that created their progenitors. Rather, these magical rodents cooperate with the residents of the City of Doors, whether by simply living together or by pursuing greater ambitions.

name: Cranium Rat Squeaker Swarm
lore: The cranium rats squeakers of Sigil have no connection to the mind flayers that created their progenitors. Rather, these magical rodents cooperate with the residents of the City of Doors, whether by simply living together or by pursuing greater ambitions.

name: Crawling Claw
lore: Crawling claws are the severed hands of murderers animated by dark magic so that they can go on killing. Wizards and warlocks of a dark bent use crawling claws as extra hands in their labors.

name: Creeper
lore: A creeper is a green, armless creature that emerges in darkness and silently prowls the Overworld on its four short legs. Its peculiar face bears no clue of its motives, but its destructiveness makes it one of the greatest threats to both life and property in the Overworld.

name: Creepy Doll
lore: Many of the evils that plague humanity on Innistrad arise from within humanity itself, and among the most terrible of these are creatures built by human hands. Some of these are animated by the magic of {@creature necro-alchemist|PSI|necro-alchemists} or witches, but others are inhabited and given life by hostile spirits or other malign forces.
languages: speaks and understands the languages known by its creator

name: Criosphinx
lore: A rare few {@creature Amonkhet sphinx|PSA|sphinxes} have the heads of rams instead of the humanlike faces of other {@creature Amonkhet sphinx|PSA|sphinxes}. Though they lie under the same curse as all their kind, they are less enigmatic and more aggressive, actively attacking {@creature angel of Amonkhet|PSA|angels}, demons, and other agents of Bolas they encounter in the desert.

name: Crokek'toeck
lore: Crokek'toeck is Yeenoghu's demonic pet. Resembling a giant mudskipper with the teeth, fur, ears, and laugh of a hyena, this unique demon can hold smaller demons in its gullet and disgorge them anywhere Yeenoghu wishes.

name: Crushing Wave Priest
lore: Crushing Wave priests are the fanatical core of the cult. Gathering in secretive cabals, they perform dark rituals to glorify Olhydra as the embodiment of water's destructiveness.

name: Crushing Wave Reaver
lore: The foot soldiers of the Crushing Wave cult are vicious sea reavers. Many of them were pirates before they fell in with the cult, and they remain eager for blood and plunder.

name: Cryonax
lore: By all the gods! How the ranger hasn't gotten himself killed is beyond my understanding.

name: Crystal Cave Merfolk
lore: The Crystal Cave merfolk have long, flowing fins and silvery scales. They have lovely, melodic voices that are enhanced by the vibrating crystals of the cave in which they dwell.

name: Crystal Dragon Wyrmling
lore: Shimmering with radiant energy and brimming with life, crystal dragons enjoy an innate psionic connection to the Positive Plane that suffuses their bodies as well as their personalities with light. Though they prefer to live in desolate, frigid regions, many of them are among the friendliest of dragonkind, nurturing and optimistic.

name: Crystal Greatwyrm
traits: Gem Awakening (Recharges after a Short or Long Rest)
actions: Mass Telekinesis (Gem Awakening Only; Recharges after a Short or Long Rest)

name: Cult Fanatic
lore: Fanatics are often part of a cult's leadership, using their charisma and dogma to influence and prey on those of weak will.

name: Cultist
lore: Cultists swear allegiance to dark powers such as elemental princes, demon lords, or archdevils. Most conceal their loyalties to avoid being ostracized, imprisoned, or executed for their beliefs.

name: Cultist of Demogorgon
lore: Cultists use extreme measures to spread radical beliefs. They often follow obscure mystical traditions or obsess over interpretations of ancient prophecies.

name: Cuprilach Rilmani
lore: Cuprilachs infiltrate places of power throughout the multiverse, serving as spies and assassins. They strike surgically, following the orders of aurumachs without question or emotion.

name: Curate
lore: A curate earns a special connection to the divine through service to shrines and temples. This devotion endows these spiritual leaders with the power to heal injuries, repel evil, and raise the dead.

name: Cyclops
lore: Cyclopes are one-eyed giants that eke out a meager existence in wild lands. Isolationists by nature, they avoid contact with other races and try to drive away strangers in their territory.

name: Daask Bruiser
lore: Intimidating and powerful, bruisers lead Daask operations when more violent and decisive shows of force are needed. These enforcers relish the brutality of their work.

name: Daask Raider
lore: Raiders use guerrilla tactics to ambush warehouses and beat down opponents. These skilled fistfighters work in tandem to claim goods and territory for Daask.

name: Dabus
lore: Among the bustling throngs of Sigil's streets float gray-skinned, vaguely humanlike figures with curled horns. Where they travel, cracks in the mortar seal shut, stray bricks float back into place, and ruined city blocks are restored.
languages: understands all languages but can't speak; communicates via Symbol Speech

name: Daemogoth
lore: Slipping through the darkest corners of the world, daemogoths are powerful fiendish creatures that feed on misery and other negative emotions. Some sages refer to daemogoths as tear lickers, owing to the fiends' tendency to lurk near battlefields and other sites of great tragedy to consume the anguish saturating the area.

name: Daemogoth Titan
lore: Daemogoth titans are towering monsters that blight the land around them. A daemogoth grows in power over the course of decades spent feeding on sorrow and draining life from nature.

name: Dagryn
lore: I've long pursued the story of the dwarf who could change into a dragon. That Minsc and his ridiculous pet could track him down first is infuriating!

name: Dandylion
lore: {@note Created by Jones D.-D.

name: Dankwood Duergar
lore: Dankwood duergar live in the deep caves below and are constantly on the search for treasure and magical discoveries they can claim as their own. Like their dwarven relatives, the Dankwood duergar are masters at crafting, but never seem to be happy or satisfied with their creations.

name: Dankwood Grung
lore: Dankwood grungs come in a variety of colors and play different roles in their community:

name: Dankwood Hag
lore: Dankwood hags are the worst kind of hags in the world. They are creepy and grumpy with long fingers that end in iron claws.

name: Dardew
lore: Dardew the Banished is a devilishly charming showman with fiery ambition. He knows how to delight an audience, even if it means making a few infernal pacts on the sly for a truly juicy spectacle.

name: Darien
lore: Typically appearing as a beautiful, young elven woman clad in golden jewelry, Darien is usually accompanied by several scantily clad and equally beautiful "bodyguards", all of whom seem to be comfortable in any climate, no matter how cold. However, in reality Darien is a powerful half-ogre illusionist and the golden jewelry aren't simply valuable trinkets, but the soul circlets of skeleton warriors—the magically disguised bodyguards that accompany her.

name: Dark Tide Knight
lore: Dark Tide knights are the elite warriors of the Crushing Wave cult. By drawing upon Olhydra's dark power, they gain the ability to ride sea-creatures that normally wouldn't be suitable as mounts, and can even breathe underwater and share their mount's senses.

name: Darkling
sourceFull: Mordenkainen Presents: Monsters of the Multiverse

name: Darkling Elder
sourceFull: Mordenkainen Presents: Monsters of the Multiverse

name: Darkmantle
lore: A darkmantle clings to cavern ceilings, remaining perfectly still as it waits for creatures to pass beneath it. From a distance, it can pass itself off as a stalactite or a lump of stone.

name: Darkweaver
lore: Arachnid predators of the Shadowfell, darkweavers inhabit caves, dungeons, and other dark locales throughout the multiverse, including Undersigil and the windswept darkness of Pandemonium. A darkweaver lurks in the shadows of its lair, waiting for hapless prey to pass by.

name: Davil Starsong
lore: Within the Waterdeep division of the Black Network, Davil is accorded the title of Master of Opportunities and Negotiations because he's good at sniffing out lucrative business deals, and he makes friends easily. Like many sun elves, Davil has an affinity for magic and is gifted with the kind of patience that comes with a long life span.

name: Dead Three Scion
lore: {@i Inheritor of a Malevolent Deific Triumvirate} Some wicked mortals have bound their souls to Bane, Bhaal, and Myrkul and gained a portion of malevolent divine power from each. A scion of the Dead Three might have seized this power in a sinister ritual or had it thrust on them by an evil Artifact or a god's malevolent will.

name: Deadbark Dryad
lore: When a dryad fails to protect its wilderness home from a great evil and is unable to forgive itself, the dryad might transform into a wicked Fey monster called a deadbark dryad. Such dryads relinquish any compassion they once felt for living beings and instead harbor hatred for anyone who dares to invade their rotted demesne.

name: Death Dog
lore: A death dog is an ugly two-headed hound that roams plains, deserts, and the Underdark. Hate burns in a death dog's heart, and a taste for humanoid flesh drives it to attack travelers and explorers.

name: Death Embrace
lore: Drifting through the dark waters of the Netherdeep, the death embrace is an ominous sight. Beneath its bell hangs a lacy, tendrilous mass that thrums with magic, surrounded by six 60-foot-long, barbed tentacles.

name: Death Giant Reaper
sourceFull: Bigby Presents: Glory of the Giants

name: Death Giant Shrouded One
sourceFull: Bigby Presents: Glory of the Giants

name: Death Kiss
sourceFull: Mordenkainen Presents: Monsters of the Multiverse
lore: A death kiss is a lesser beholder that can come into being when a true beholder has a vivid nightmare about losing blood. Its coloration and shape resemble those of the beholder that dreamed it into existence, but its hue is more muted, and instead of magical eye rays, it has ten long tentacles, each ending in a mouth full of teeth.

name: Death Knight
lore: When a paladin that falls from grace dies without seeking atonement, dark powers can transform the once-mortal knight into a hateful undead creature. A death knight is a skeletal warrior clad in fearsome plate armor.

name: Death Tyrant
lore: On rare occasions, a beholder's sleeping mind drifts to places beyond its normal madness, imagining a reality in which it exists beyond death. When such dreams take hold, a beholder can transform, its flesh sloughing away to leave a death tyrant behind.

name: Death's Head
lore: A death's head is a disembodied, flying head. The type of creature one of these grotesque undead originated from determines how it terrorizes it prey.

name: Death's Head of Bhaal
lore: Bhaal's cultists emulate their deity, carrying out gruesome murders to spread fear and horror. They are charming and disarming when they wish, but in combat their true, bloodthirsty nature comes to the fore.

name: Deathless Rider
lore: Legends told in the courts of Eldraine suggest that the deathless riders serve a warlock-queen, known simply as the Shadow Queen, who rules from a castle called Dynnistan. It isn't known whether these legends are based on any true experience or are simply the result of imagination trying to account for a terrifying and unexplained phenomenon.
languages: understands the languages it knew in life but can't speak

name: Deathlock
sourceFull: Mordenkainen Presents: Monsters of the Multiverse
languages: the languages it knew in life

name: Deathlock Mastermind
sourceFull: Mordenkainen Presents: Monsters of the Multiverse
languages: the languages it knew in life

name: Deathlock Wight
sourceFull: Mordenkainen Presents: Monsters of the Multiverse
languages: the languages it knew in life

name: Deathpact Angel
lore: Deathpact angels dwell in the grandest of Orzhov cathedrals, where they surround themselves with wealth and wretched vassals that are utterly in their thrall. Posing as a beneficent god, a deathpact angel attracts petitioners who beg the angel for blessings: wealth, prestige, health, revenge, and the like.

name: Deathwolf
lore: A deathwolf combines the might of a werewolf, the unholy powers of undeath, and the magic of the full moon. Deathwolves are most common on Krynn but occasionally are found in other locations where lycanthropy and necromancy are prevalent.

name: Decapus
lore: Decapuses are carnivorous, solitary hunters that swing through trees, scooping up prey with their 10-foot-long, suckered tentacles. Decapuses also use their tentacles to climb walls and ceilings.

name: Deep Crow
lore: The only warning you'll get is that fearsome, far-off caw, echoing from the darkness like a death knell. For by the time those glowing red eyes fix on you, it's far too late to run...

name: Deep Gnome (Svirfneblin)
lore: Deep gnomes, or svirfneblin, live far below the world's surface in twisting warrens and sculpted caverns. They survive by virtue of their stealth, cleverness, and tenacity.

name: Deep Rothé
sourceFull: Mordenkainen Presents: Monsters of the Multiverse

name: Deep Scion
sourceFull: Mordenkainen Presents: Monsters of the Multiverse
lore: Deep scions began life as people who were stolen from shore or saved from sinking ships and offered a terrible bargain by an undersea power: surrender, body and soul, or drown. Those who submit are subjected to an ancient ritual widespread among evil aquatic creatures.
actions: Psychic Screech (Hybrid Form Only; Recharges after a Short or Long Rest)

name: Deepking Horgar Steelshadow V
lore: Deepking Horgar V ascended to the throne in 1372 DR. He is a ruthless and canny ruler who engages in secret meetings with all the city's councils and clans to keep their members guessing what he's up to, hands out favors out of the blue, and lets it be known that he'll use any trick to maintain his hold on power.
actions: Enlarge (Recharges after a Short or Long Rest)
Invisibility (Recharges after a Short or Long Rest)
Leadership (Recharges after a Short or Long Rest)

name: Degloth
lore: Degloths are massive, blue, bipedal demons with razor-studded fists. They are commonly used as shock troops on the front lines of wars waged in the Abyss and other Outer Planes.

name: Deinonychus
sourceFull: Mordenkainen Presents: Monsters of the Multiverse
lore: This larger cousin of the velociraptor kills by gripping its target with its claws and feeding.

name: Demilich
lore: The immortality granted to a lich lasts only as long as it feeds mortal souls to its phylactery. If it falters or fails in that task, its bones turn to dust until only its skull remains.

name: Demobat
lore: Demobats are slimy, winged pests endemic to demonic realms. A demobat has four serrated tentacles, which trail behind it during flight, and its eyeless head opens wide to reveal a toothed maw.

name: Demodog
lore: Demodogs are sinister, quadrupedal predators that spawn from limacine larvae. From a distance, they loosely resemble hairless dogs with tough, wrinkled hides and faceless, flower-like heads.

name: Demodragon
lore: Once the prized pet of Demogorgon, Prince of Demons, the demodragon now resides in the Nine Hells, where it serves as the gatekeeper of the Hellfire Games' final challenge. Raised by vile magic from the corpse of a beheaded black dragon, the demodragon is the picture of its creator: an abomination with three writhing tails, monstrous wings, and two hideous heads.

name: Demogorgon
lore: A predator of the Upside Down, the demogorgon hunts the dimension looking for unfortunate creatures that find their way there.

name: Demogorgon Spawn
lore: Monstrous offspring of the bestial demon lord Demogorgon, these spawn are lean, bipedal creatures with bulbous, featureless heads. A Demogorgon spawn's head opens like a flower in bloom, revealing a gaping maw and five or more fleshy petals lined with teeth.

name: Demos Magen
sourceFull: Icewind Dale: Rime of the Frostmaiden
lore: Demos magen wear armor, wield weapons, and typically serve as guards. Magen are magical, humanlike beings created by a wizard spell (see the {@spell create magen|IDRotF} spell in appendix D) or by other arcane methods.
languages: understands the languages of its creator but can't speak

name: Dermot Wurder (Tier 1)
lore: When you're the twelfth son of a poor goblin family in Jigow, the only way to make a name for yourself is to become a great champion—someone who can win bragging contests in the local taverns night after night. Young Dermot Wurder, however, wasn't interested in performing feats of strength or agility that would win him a boast-worthy epithet.

name: Dermot Wurder (Tier 2)
lore: When you're the twelfth son of a poor goblin family in Jigow, the only way to make a name for yourself is to become a great champion—someone who can win bragging contests in the local taverns night after night. Young Dermot Wurder, however, wasn't interested in performing feats of strength or agility that would win him a boast-worthy epithet.

name: Dermot Wurder (Tier 3)
lore: When you're the twelfth son of a poor goblin family in Jigow, the only way to make a name for yourself is to become a great champion—someone who can win bragging contests in the local taverns night after night. Young Dermot Wurder, however, wasn't interested in performing feats of strength or agility that would win him a boast-worthy epithet.

name: Derro
sourceFull: Mordenkainen Presents: Monsters of the Multiverse
lore: Mind flayers must be stopped. They have visited horrors on countless worlds, and entire groups of people have been mutated by illithid experiments.

name: Derro Apprentice
lore: Derro who have just begun to discover and control their magic are called apprentices. The magic of a derro apprentice is dangerous and unpredictable.

name: Derro Raider
lore: Hostility and greed are guiding values for some derro. These derro indiscriminately raid settlements and rob travelers regardless of the apparent value of any goods they steal.

name: Derro Savant
sourceFull: Mordenkainen Presents: Monsters of the Multiverse

name: Derwyth
lore: Derwyth lives in Cernant Valley—a forested basin at the foot of the Tegefed Mountains—along with many animals, most of which are small woodland creatures. These animals act as Derwyth's eyes and ears, quickly bringing her news of events in the wood, particularly the arrival and actions of strangers.

name: Deva
lore: An angel is a celestial agent sent forth into the planes to further its god's agenda for weal or woe. Its sublime beauty and presence can drive awestruck onlookers to their knees.

name: Devkarin Lich
lore: Powerful spellcasters of the Devkarin elves, steeped in Golgari magic, can transcend death to become liches. For them, life and death don't merely chase each other in an inevitable cycle; the two can intersect, and at that nexus the liches find immense power, which commands the awe, envy, and fear of other Golgari.

name: Devourer
sourceFull: Mordenkainen Presents: Monsters of the Multiverse
lore: Of all the abominations unleashed by {@creature Orcus|MPMM}, devourers are among the most feared. These tall, mummy-like Undead wander the planes, consuming souls and spreading Orcus's creed of replacing all life with everlasting death.

name: Dhergoloth
sourceFull: Mordenkainen Presents: Monsters of the Multiverse
lore: A dhergoloth's head doesn't turn along with its furiously spinning torso, and its torso can spin a different direction from its dancing legs. I'd like to vivisect one a some point to find out how this can be.

name: Dimetrodon
sourceFull: Mordenkainen Presents: Monsters of the Multiverse
lore: This sail-backed reptile is commonly found in areas where dinosaurs live. It hunts on shores and in shallow water, filling a similar role to a crocodile.

name: Dire Corby
lore: Heralded by the ominous, deep rumbling of their strange song, great packs of dire corbies range across the Underdark, stripping the tunnels and caverns they traverse of all living things. Even the mightiest creatures of the Underdark hide within fortified lairs or step aside from a dire corby flock, knowing of how these creatures' bizarre song disrupts, disorients, and ultimately overwhelms their prey.

name: Dire Troll
sourceFull: Mordenkainen Presents: Monsters of the Multiverse

name: Dirt-Under-Nails
traits: Dark One's Own Luck (Recharges after a Short or Long Rest)
Special Equipment (+2 Rod of the Pact Keeper)

name: Dispater
lore: Despite frequent shifts in leadership among other archdevils, Dispater has maintained his position as the ruler of Dis since its creation. Known for his vigilance and caution, he rarely leaves his tower, and even less frequently leaves the Iron City.

name: Displacer Beast
lore: This monstrous predator takes its name from its ability to displace light so that it appears to be several feet away from its actual location. A displacer beast resembles a sleek great cat covered in blue-black fur.

name: Displacer Fiend
lore: Long ago a Fey lord led his hunt into the Nine Hells to settle a score with an archfiend over mortals claimed by both. What fate the lord met is unknown, but of all his expedition, one particular faerie breed prospered in its new environment.

name: Diviner
lore: Diviners are specialist wizards who know that knowledge is power. They might act aloof and mysterious, hinting at omens and secrets, or they might be know-it-alls, spilling secrets and insights to advance their own status or reputation.
traits: Portent (Recharges after the Diviner Casts a Divination Spell of 1st Level or Higher)

name: Diviner Wizard
sourceFull: Mordenkainen Presents: Monsters of the Multiverse

name: Dohwar
lore: Dohwars are short, pudgy, flightless avians that look and move like penguins. Dohwars migrate from the Feywild to Wildspace, where they are typically encountered.

name: Dolgaunt
sourceFull: Eberron: Rising from the Last War
lore: Dolgaunts are emaciated hobgoblins with disease-hued flesh. Their eye sockets gape open and empty above a mouth with a wormlike tongue.

name: Dolgrim
sourceFull: Eberron: Rising from the Last War
lore: Dolgrims are squat, deformed things. Warped by the daelkyr, a dolgrim is essentially two goblins crushed into one creature, their misshapen body boasting four arms and a pair of twisted mouths that gibber and slather at the front of a headless torso.

name: Dolphin
sourceFull: Mordenkainen Presents: Monsters of the Multiverse

name: Dolphin Delighter
sourceFull: Mordenkainen Presents: Monsters of the Multiverse

name: Don-Jon Raskin
lore: Adventurers who undertake the Mountain's Toe Quest meet Don-Jon Raskin, a fearless troubleshooter who has experienced more than his fair share of adventures.
traits: Not Dead Yet (Recharges after a Long Rest)

name: Donaar Blit'zen
lore: Everybody cooooool out! The hulking dragonborn decisionist of the "C" Team, Donaar Blit'zen is an icon of brash confidence and warrior chill.
traits: Champion Challenge (Recharges after a Short or Long Rest)

name: Doomguard Doom Lord
lore: Doom lords are the highest-ranking members of the Doomguard, embodying the multiverse's inevitable collapse into dust. Doom lords' mere presence snuffs the life from weaker foes, and the stoutest walls crumble before them.

name: Doomguard Rot Blade
lore: Rot blades are the entropic levers through which the Doomguard quickens the natural decay of the multiverse. These members wield weapons immersed in necrotic energy, which can turn their enemies to ash.

name: Doomwake Giant
lore: Belief is a powerful force in Theros, capable of bringing about wonders and changing the world—but not always for the better. Doomwake giants arise from pernicious fears that infect a whole city or region.

name: Doppelganger
lore: Doppelgangers are devious shapeshifters that take on the appearance of other humanoids, throwing off pursuit or luring victims to their doom with misdirection and disguise. Few creatures spread fear, suspicion, and deceit better than doppelgangers.

name: Doric
lore: The tiefling Doric was taken in by a wood elf enclave in Neverwinter Wood after being abandoned as a child, and she guards her adoptive community with nature's ferocity. The Circle of the Moon has taught her to heed her primal instincts, which rarely lead her astray.

name: Dracohydra
lore: The dracohydra is the result of arcane experimentation dedicated to recreating Tiamat's power. Amalgamating the magic of chromatic dragons with the blood of a hydra resulted in a many-headed draconic monster with wings and multiple snakelike tails.

name: Dracolich
lore: {@i Draconic Tyrant Reborn} The vilest dragons seek to escape the grip of death, employing ageless secrets and blasphemous magic to become horrors called dracoliches. These deathless dragons bind their spirits to gems and magically animate their rotting corpses.

name: Draconian Dreadnought
lore: The largest of the draconians are the dreadnoughts, who are born from the eggs of silver, blue, or sapphire dragons. They fly on mighty wings over the battlefield to wherever the fighting is the thickest.

name: Draconian Foot Soldier
lore: The most numerous draconians are the foot soldiers, who are born from brass, white, or crystal dragon eggs. They are the smallest of their kind, and their slight wings are incapable of flight—though they do afford the draconians some control when falling.

name: Draconian Infiltrator
lore: Copper, black, and topaz dragon eggs yield these sly and stealthy draconians, who often serve their creators as scouts and spies. They use the paralytic venom of their saliva to coat their weapons, making them formidable assassins as well.

name: Draconian Mage
lore: Draconians born from the eggs of bronze, green, and emerald dragons have some ability to wield magic. They often lead small groups of draconian foot soldiers, using their magic to snipe across the battlefield or aid their allies' incursions and attacks.

name: Draconian Mastermind
lore: The rarest and most powerful of the draconians are the masterminds—spellcasters and strategists who most often serve as military commanders or as advisors to those who created them. They emerge from gold, red, or amethyst dragon eggs, wingless but possessed of an arsenal of eldritch power.

name: Draconic Shard
lore: Gem dragons wield psionic power, and when a powerful gem dragon perishes, the force of the dragon's will and mind sometimes refuses to pass on. This essence lingers in the form of a psychic remnant called a draconic shard.

name: Draegloth
sourceFull: Mordenkainen Presents: Monsters of the Multiverse
lore: A draegloth is a demon created by an elf priest of Lolth in an unholy, dangerous ritual in which it is infused with the fey essence of the creator and the fiendish essence of a {@creature glabrezu}. This ritual rarely succeeds, but Lolth's faithful consider it worth the risk, as the resulting creature is gifted with innate magic and physical might.

name: Dragon Army Dragonnel
sourceFull: Dragonlance: Shadow of the Dragon Queen
lore: Dragonnels from the volcanic mountains surrounding Sanction are closely related to red dragons, resembling them in scale coloration and general shape. They are cruel and selfish creatures trained by the Dragon Armies to serve as mounts for trusted officers.
languages: understands Common and Draconic but can't speak

name: Dragon Army Officer
sourceFull: Dragonlance: Shadow of the Dragon Queen
lore: The Dragon Armies gather fanatical followers to their ranks. Recruits are indoctrinated to revere the Dragon Queen and view dragons as her favored servants.

name: Dragon Army Soldier
sourceFull: Dragonlance: Shadow of the Dragon Queen
lore: The Dragon Armies gather fanatical followers to their ranks. Recruits are indoctrinated to revere the Dragon Queen and view dragons as her favored servants.

name: Dragon Blessed
lore: Dragon blessed are the acolytes of dragons, whom they revere as gods. They wield magic to heal and support those who have earned their dragon masters' favor—and scourge those who incur the dragons' wrath.

name: Dragon Chosen
lore: Dragon chosen are mighty warriors who offer their bodies as vessels for a dragon's might. They serve as enforcers of their masters' will; they often accompany dragon speakers—and are quick to defend them if they sense any ill intent.

name: Dragon Speaker
lore: Dragon speakers are charismatic and persuasive orators who serve as a dragon's ambassadors among other folk. Dragon speakers have loud and authoritative voices, which they use to gain valuable resources, diplomatic connections, and donations of treasure and magic for their dragons—as well as to weave magic both subtle and thundering.

name: Dragon Turtle
lore: Dragon turtles are among the most fearsome creatures of the oceans. As large and voracious as the oldest of its land-based dragon kin, a dragon turtle strikes with its deadly jaws, steaming breath, and crushing tail.

name: Dragon Turtle Wyrmling
lore: Dragon turtles are mighty creatures whose inherent magic is intimately linked with the oceans of the Material Plane. The dragon turtle presented in the {@i Monster Manual} is a typical adult, at least a century old and boasting a valuable hoard.

name: Dragonbait
lore: Dragonbait is a champion of good and a saurial-a race that originated on a distant world and whose members have long lives. Very few saurials dwell in the Forgotten Realms, and no saurial communities are believed to exist anywhere in the world.

name: Dragonblood Ooze
lore: Magic-minded artisans have long incorporated parts of dragons' bodies into magic items, crafting dragon hide into armor and forging weapons from claws and teeth. Alchemists have found beneficial uses for dragon blood, but ill-advised experiments have also given rise to dragonblood oozes.
languages: understands Draconic and the languages of its creator but can't speak

name: Dragonbone Golem
lore: A dragonbone golem is composed of dragon bones linked together with adamantine wire into the form of a dragon, animated by drawing on the bones' inherent magic. Most dragon bone golems are created by powerful dragons from the bones of vanquished rivals.
languages: understands Draconic and the languages of its creator but can't speak

name: Dragonborn of Bahamut
lore: Often called platinum knights, dragonborn champions of Bahamut might belong to an order that exists to protect the world from evil—and especially from evil dragons. A champion of Bahamut has a distinctive pair of platinum wings and a breath weapon suffused with radiant energy.

name: Dragonborn of Sardior
lore: Sardior is said to have died in the destruction of the First World. But many believe that Sardior's divine essence survives within every gem dragon.

name: Dragonborn of Tiamat
lore: Champions of the dragon queen are often called Talons of Tiamat. They work tirelessly to free their master from her imprisonment in Avernus, and Tiamat grants these pawns immense strength and a devastating breath weapon.

name: Dragonflesh Abomination
lore: A dragonflesh abomination is the final stage of a grafter's transformation—a hulking monster whose corrupted brain is ablaze with desire for treasure. Its abhorrent transformation erases the last vestiges of its previous life.
languages: understands Common and Draconic but can't speak

name: Dragonflesh Grafter
lore: An ordinary dragonflesh grafter is a hulking fusion of draconic and bipedal characteristics, standing between 8 and 12 feet tall. As its original nature wanes, its draconic powers grow, giving it a thick layer of dragon scale armor that covers the grafted areas of its body.

name: Dragonnel
lore: Dragonnels are distantly related to chromatic, gem, and metallic dragons and resemble them in basic form. Intelligent enough to understand speech but incapable of speaking themselves, they are willful creatures motivated by the desire for food and entertainment.
languages: understands Draconic and Common but can't speak

name: Drannin Splithelm
lore: Drannin is a self-serving dwarf who has always felt that, due to his heritage, he was owed better than he received. When he didn't receive the accolades, he began to see everyone in his way as the problem, trying to keep him down, and he vowed to do whatever it took to turn the tables.
traits: Action Surge (Recharges after a Short or Long Rest)
Indomitable (Recharges after a Short or Long Rest)
Second Wind (Recharges after a Short or Long Rest)

name: Dread Doppelganger
lore: Let me borrow your face... You won't be needing it anymore.

name: Dread Warrior
lore: The Red Wizards (Dead in Thay) make use of several kinds of undead minions, including the special servants known as dread warriors. After being created by a secret ritual, a dread warrior is further enchanted so that a Red Wizard can employ the creature in the fashion of a spellcaster's familiar.

name: Dream Eater
lore: Originating from the dream-warped elven realm of Silvanesti, dream eaters are violent manifestations of nightmares and subconscious terrors. The true appearance of a dream eater is obscured, as the dream eater twists its hazy form into surreal illusions of its foes' greatest fears.

name: Dreaming Dark Infiltrator
lore: Dreaming Dark Infiltrators are spies sent to gather intelligence. They plant psychic seeds in their minds of their foes that enhance the infiltrators' combat prowess against those enemies.

name: Dreaming Dark Mindkiller
lore: Mindkillers are the assassins of the Dreaming Dark, sent to terrify their foes before striking to kill. Mindkillers force foes to attack their own allies, causing physical and emotional devastation to grow the power of the Dreaming Dark.

name: Drelnza
lore: Drelnza is the vampire daughter of Iggwilv. She is the Witch Queen's greatest treasure, an immortal warrior who guards some of her mother's most precious belongings decades after the archmage's mysterious departure.

name: Dretch
languages: telepathy 60 ft. (works only with creatures that understand Abyssal)

name: Drider
lore: When a drow shows great promise, Lolth summons it to the Demonweb Pits for a test of faith and strength. Those that pass the test rise higher in the Spider Queen's favor.

name: Droki
lore: Droki hates everyone in general and surfacers in particular. He gnashes his teeth if he has to address the characters, taking every opportunity to be insulting and misleading.

name: Drow
lore: Tens of thousands of years ago, the elves were divided, with those of benevolent disposition battling those that were selfish and cruel. The war among elvenkind ended when the good elves banished their malevolent kin to the subterranean depths.

name: Drow Arachnomancer
sourceFull: Mordenkainen Presents: Monsters of the Multiverse
lore: Drow spellcasters who seek to devote themselves wholly to {@deity Lolth|Drow|MTF}, the Spider Queen, sometimes walk the sinister path of the arachnomancer. By offering up body and soul to Lolth, they gain tremendous power and a supernatural connection to the ancient spiders of the Demonweb Pits, channeling magic from that dread place.

name: Drow Favored Consort
sourceFull: Mordenkainen Presents: Monsters of the Multiverse
lore: Nearly every priestess of {@deity Lolth|Drow|MTF}, including the powerful {@creature drow matron mother|MPMM} in this book, takes an attractive drow as consort. Chosen as much for beauty as for magical might, a drow favored consort can hold their own in both conversation and combat.

name: Drow Gunslinger
lore: Firearms aren't widely available in the North, but some members of Bregan D'aerthe are equipped with Lantanese pistols, bullets, and packets of smokepowder. These drow gunslingers are expert pistoleers, as skilled with their guns as the best archers are with their bows.

name: Drow House Captain
sourceFull: Mordenkainen Presents: Monsters of the Multiverse
lore: House captains will do anything to protect their family—whether that's their birth house or their platoon of scrappy rebels. I'd do anything for my (sometimes infuriating) mother and for my chosen family, so I admire their dedication.

name: Drow Inquisitor
sourceFull: Mordenkainen Presents: Monsters of the Multiverse
lore: Lolth's worshipers expect treachery—the Spider Queen encourages it, after all. A certain amount of backstabbing and double-crossing can be managed, but too much can undermine an entire community.

name: Drow Matron Mother
sourceFull: Mordenkainen Presents: Monsters of the Multiverse
lore: Among drow followers of {@deity Lolth|Drow|MTF}, each noble house is led by a matron mother, an influential priestess of Lolth charged with carrying out the god's will while also advancing the interests of the family. Matron mothers embody the scheming and treachery associated with the Queen of Spiders.

name: Drow Shadowblade
sourceFull: Mordenkainen Presents: Monsters of the Multiverse
lore: Drow shadowblades steal down the dim passages of the Underdark, bound on errands of mayhem. They protect enclaves and Underdark cities from enemies and track down thieves who make off with prized treasures.

name: Drowned Ascetic
lore: Dressed in tattered robes, its fists wrapped in long rib bons of rotting cloth, the drowned ascetic moves with alarming speed for an undead creature. This martial artist, rising from the ocean in Tammeraut's Fate, retains its fighting reflexes despite its rotting flesh.
languages: understands the languages it knew in life but can't speak

name: Drowned Assassin
lore: The drowned assassin stalks and kills in a grim pantomime of its former occupation. Sneaking from shadow to shadow, and appearing in the undead assault of Tammeraut's Fate, it aims its barnacle-encrusted hand crossbow with deadly precision.
languages: understands the languages it knew in life but can't speak

name: Drowned Blade
lore: Assaulting the hermitage in Tammeraut's Fate, this barnacle-encrusted undead warrior fights with surprising cunning. Starfish cling to its wispy beard, and its evil rage is visible in its bloated gray eyes.
languages: understands the languages it knew in life but can't speak

name: Drowned Master
lore: This waterlogged undead, bound to the Pit of Hatred in Tammeraut's Fate, hovers menacingly over the bones of its victims. Its torso, arms, and head retain their former shapes, but its legs have split into shadowy tentacles.
languages: understands the languages it knew in life but can't speak

name: Druid
lore: Druids dwell in forests and other secluded wilderness locations, where they protect the natural world from monsters and the encroachment of civilization. Some are tribal shamans who heal the sick, pray to animal spirits, and provide spiritual guidance.

name: Druid of the Old Ways
lore: The druids of the Old Ways are the keepers of ancient Gruul traditions devoted to the primal ferocity of animal gods such as Ilharg the Raze-Boar and Kashath the Stalker. The druids of the Old Ways believe that civilization will never be eradicated by scattered raids and petty skirmishes.

name: Dryad
lore: Travelers entering a forest might catch a glimpse of a feminine form flitting through the trees. Warm laughter hangs on the air, drawing those who hear it deeper into the emerald shadows.

name: Duergar
lore: The tyrannical duergar, also known as gray dwarves, dwell in fantastic cities deep in the Underdark. Using ancient dwarven knowledge and myriad slaves, they work tirelessly to expand their subterranean kingdoms.
actions: Enlarge (Recharges after a Short or Long Rest)
Invisibility (Recharges after a Short or Long Rest)

name: Duergar Darkhaft
actions: Enlarge (Recharges after a Short or Long Rest)
Invisibility (Recharges after a Short or Long Rest)

name: Duergar Despot
sourceFull: Mordenkainen Presents: Monsters of the Multiverse

name: Duergar Hammerer
sourceFull: Mordenkainen Presents: Monsters of the Multiverse

name: Duergar Kavalrachni
sourceFull: Mordenkainen Presents: Monsters of the Multiverse
actions: Shared Invisibility (Recharges after a Short or Long Rest)

name: Duergar Keeper of the Flame
actions: Enlarge (Recharges after a Short or Long Rest)
Invisibility (Recharges after a Short or Long Rest)

name: Duergar Mind Master
sourceFull: Mordenkainen Presents: Monsters of the Multiverse

name: Duergar Screamer
sourceFull: Mordenkainen Presents: Monsters of the Multiverse

name: Duergar Soulblade
sourceFull: Mordenkainen Presents: Monsters of the Multiverse
actions: Invisibility (Recharges after a Short or Long Rest)

name: Duergar Spy
lore: The tyrannical duergar, also known as gray dwarves, dwell in fantastic cities deep in the Underdark. Using ancient dwarven knowledge and myriad slaves, they work tirelessly to expand their subterranean kingdoms.
actions: Enlarge (Recharges after a Short or Long Rest)
Invisibility (Recharges after a Short or Long Rest)

name: Duergar Stone Guard
sourceFull: Mordenkainen Presents: Monsters of the Multiverse
actions: Invisibility (Recharges after a Short or Long Rest)

name: Duergar Warlord
sourceFull: Mordenkainen Presents: Monsters of the Multiverse

name: Duergar Xarrorn
sourceFull: Mordenkainen Presents: Monsters of the Multiverse
actions: Invisibility (Recharges after a Short or Long Rest)

name: Duke Thalamra Vanthampur
lore: Acid-tongued, shrewd, and aggressive, Duke Thalamra Vanthampur is the matriarch of the Vanthampur family. Born with nothing, she spent years wallowing in obscurity, repairing and renovating the city's ancient sewer system.

name: Duke Zalto
lore: Master crafters and organized warriors, fire giants dwell among volcanoes, lava floes, and rocky mountains. They are ruthless militaristic brutes whose mastery of metalwork is legendary.

name: Dullahan
lore: Dullahans are headless undead warriors—the remains of villains who let vengeance consume them. These decapitated hunters haunt the areas where they were slain, butchering innocents in search of their severed heads or to quench their thirst for revenge.
traits: Headless Summoning (Recharges after a Short or Long Rest)
languages: understands the languages it knew in life but can't speak

name: Dum-Dum Goblin
lore: Dum Dum goblins are your average run-of-the-mill goblins that live in the Dankwood. They are led by their chief, Grobble, who loves to eat mushroom pie and send out his gang of goblin thieves to steal stuff.

name: Dunbarrow Witch
lore: "Taste the sweetness, breath for breath. Keep the balance, death for death.

name: Durnan
lore: {@i Human proprietor} I'll give you a free flagon of ale if you don't talk to me about the weather. Durnan is the owner and proprietor of the Yawning Portal.

name: Durnn
lore: War horns sound, stones fly from catapults, and the thunder of a thousand booted feet echoes across the land as hobgoblins march to battle. Across the borderlands of civilization, settlements and settlers must contend with these aggressive humanoids, whose thirst for conquest is never satisfied.

name: Dusk Hag
sourceFull: Eberron: Rising from the Last War
lore: Dusk hags resemble gnarled crones with shriveled orange skin, tangled gray hair, and eyes that burn like hot coals. They see visions of the future in their dreams, and their dark magic allows them to influence the dreams of others, sending messages or inflicting nightmares with a touch.

name: Dust Hulk
sourceFull: Bigby Presents: Glory of the Giants
lore: Dust hulks are descended from enclaves of stone giants who lived in extreme environments, isolated from others. Elemental energy from the Plane of Air eroded their physical forms and slowly transformed them into Elementals.

name: Dwarf
lore: Although there are no dwarves that live in the actual Dankwood, they will sometime pass through in travel or explore the forest for coveted materials and magical stones. They pride themselves in their skills and can create beautiful artifacts from the objects they find in nature.

name: Dwarf Skeleton
sourceFull: Phandelver and Below: The Shattered Obelisk

name: Dybbuk
sourceFull: Mordenkainen Presents: Monsters of the Multiverse
lore: Dybbuks are demons that terrorize mortals on the Material Plane by possessing corpses and giving them a semblance of life, after which the demons use them to engage in a range of sordid activities. In their natural form, dybbuks appear as translucent flying jellyfish, trailing long tentacles as they move through the air.

name: Dyrrn
sourceFull: Eberron: Rising from the Last War
lore: Dyrrn is known to its followers as the Corruptor, the Stealer of Thoughts, the Slithering Lord, and the Foul Labyrinth. In the lore of the Gatekeepers, it is said that Dyrrn plants thoughts in the weak minded—the seeds of terrible ideas that fester and grow.

name: Dzaan's Simulacrum
sourceFull: Icewind Dale: Rime of the Frostmaiden
lore: Unbeknownst to his executioners, Dzaan had used a spell scroll of simulacrum to create a copy of himself. This simulacrum dwells in the sunken Netherese spire, waiting for its creator to return.
actions: Magic Missile (1st-Level Spell; Requires a Spell Slot)

name: Earth Elemental Myrmidon
sourceFull: Mordenkainen Presents: Monsters of the Multiverse
languages: one language of its creator's choice

name: Eater of Hope
lore: An eater of hope is bitter to the core, resentful of all forms of life and joy. Although these demons can strike down most foes, they prefer to let terror and despair overtake their victims first, letting their victims marinate in fear before the fiend devours them.

name: Eater of Knowledge
lore: Originally created by the mind flayer god-brain Ilsensine and now produced by some of that god's followers, eaters of knowledge are lumbering, bipedal masses of squelching muscles and exposed brain matter. These rugose hulks collect information from others by devouring brains before returning to their masters with delicious secrets.

name: Eblis
lore: Eblis (pronounced EH-blee) are thought to be reincarnations of evil humans who were punished by gods for acts of larceny and kidnapping. These intelligent, evil cranes loom 8 feet tall and lay eggs as mundane birds do.

name: Ebondeath
lore: Over a thousand years ago, the black dragon Chardansearavitriol, commonly known as Ebondeath, settled in the Mere of Dead Men, making his lair in the ruined citadel of Uthtower. Ebondeath became a dracolich and was worshiped by the Cult of the Dragon until the death god Myrkul's influence waned across Faerûn and the dragon's bones turned to dust.
languages: Any languages it knew in life

name: Echo of Demogorgon
sourceFull: Bigby Presents: Glory of the Giants
lore: No transformation wrought by the hand (or tentacle, whatever) of a demon lord is rightly understood as a "blessing." Some legends claim the first ettins were orcs transformed by Demogorgon, the Prince of Demons, while others tie their origin to Grolantor or another god of the Ordning.

name: Edgin Darvis
lore: Edgin Darvis has a talent for improvisation and strategy. He's a musician and tale-teller who relies on charisma first—rather than magic or muscle—to escape trouble.

name: Edwin Odesseiron
lore: In many ways an archetypal Red Wizard, Edwin Odesseiron is an exceedingly arrogant conjuration mage hailing from the eastern nation of Thay. He originally traveled west to the Sword Coast on a mission to kill the Rashemi Wychlaran Dynaheir, both as part of a personal rivalry and under orders from his superior Red Wizards.

name: Egg Hunter Adult
lore: The salamander-like adult of the species can inflate its bulbous tail so that it takes on the shape and texture of a dragon egg. It uses this mimicry to establish itself in a nest tended by a dragon or other creatures.

name: Egg Hunter Hatchling
lore: A dragon egg drained by an egg hunter is filled with {@dice 1d6} new eggs, which hatch inside the dragon egg's shell in {@dice 1d6} days. The egg hunter hatchlings burst forth {@dice 1d4} days later, using the needlelike egg tooth that extends from their heads.

name: Eidolon
sourceFull: Mordenkainen Presents: Monsters of the Multiverse
lore: To protect sites they deem holy, gods often rely on eidolons, ghostly spirits bound to safeguard a sacred place. Forged from the souls of those with unwavering devotion, eidolons stalk temples and vaults to ensure that no enemy defiles, damages, or plunders these sites.
languages: the languages it knew in life

name: Elder Brain
sourceFull: Mordenkainen Presents: Monsters of the Multiverse
lore: The ultimate expression of mind flayer domination, an elder brain sprawls within a vat of viscous brine, cared for by mind flayer minions as it touches the thoughts of creatures near and far. It scrawls upon the canvas of the creatures' minds, rewriting their thoughts and authoring their dreams.

name: Elder Brain Dragon
lore: One of the few consolations available to those who must contend with a mind flayer colony is the limit of its reach, which spreads only as far as the influence of the colony's elder brain. But this small solace withers away when a colony manages to capture a dragon.

name: Elder Dinosaur
lore: The opening of Orazca, the golden city—which marks the turn between the Ixalan set and Rivals of Ixalan—reveals the existence of six huge and ancient elder dinosaurs, apparently preserved for centuries. Compared to their smaller cousins, they have less brightly-colored plumage and more grayish scales, but their feathers are a bright gold that matches the city around them.

name: Elder Oblex
sourceFull: Mordenkainen Presents: Monsters of the Multiverse

name: Elder Tempest
sourceFull: Mordenkainen Presents: Monsters of the Multiverse
lore: Terrifying storms manifest in the bodies of elder tempests. Beings carved from clouds, wind, rain, and lightning, elder tempests assume the shape of serpents that slither through the sky.

name: Eldritch Eddy
lore: When magical energy becomes dangerously unstable, it sometimes animates into an eldritch eddy: an uncontrolled whirlwind of Evocation magic that crackles with fire and lightning. Eldritch eddies typically arise where magic use is heavy and irresponsible, such as near a fanatical mage's sanctum, a workshop that churns out magic items, or a library where undertrained scribes work with Spell Scrolls.
languages: understands Common plus one other language but can't speak

name: Eldritch Lich
lore: From beyond the stars, a Great Old One whispers promises of reality-defying knowledge and world-bending power. When a wizard or a warlock hears that whisper and listens too intently, they might set foot on the twisting path toward becoming an eldritch lich.

name: Elemental Cataclysm
lore: {@i The End and Beginning of Ages} Beyond the fringes of the Elemental Planes, primordial forces endlessly clash amid the Elemental Chaos. Within the vastness and violence of this realm rage elemental cataclysms, entities spawned from the raw forces of the multiverse and awash in dissonant elemental powers.

name: Elise
lore: Dr. Mordenheim's supposed beloved and greatest achievement, Elise is a confused, frustrated soul who never wished for her current circumstances.
languages: understands the languages of its creator but can't speak

name: Elizar Dryflagon
lore: Elizar was a druid who claims to be a member of the Circle of the Scarlet Moon. However, Elizar is really in service to Imix, maintaining some druidic power through a connection to the elements.
traits: Summon Mephits (Recharges after a Long Rest)

name: Elkhorn
lore: Elkhorn has been Strongheart's most stalwart companion over the years—unflinchingly loyal, tirelessly optimistic, and fiercely devoted to the destruction of evil in all its forms. He's not especially bright, so he fights with his heart rather than his wits.

name: Ember
lore: Emperor Ariakas, Glory to the Dragon Queen's name! Our designs on Abanasinia unfold according to your plans.

name: Emerald Claw Commander
lore: Emerald Claw commanders lead large groups of soldiers, inspiring the fanatical devotion of Humanoids and Undead alike.

name: Emerald Claw Knight
lore: Knights of the Emerald Claw command squads of Humanoid or Undead soldiers. As they draw nearer to death, the chill of the grave suff uses their weapon attacks.

name: Emerald Dragon Wyrmling
lore: Emerald dragons are the most curious, cunning, and manipulative of the gem dragons, wielding psionic power to weave illusions to deceive and disorient. They cloak their own presence so they can observe other creatures without being discovered as they collect information on everything from local cultural practices to supernatural occurrences.

name: Emerald Greatwyrm
traits: Gem Awakening (Recharges after a Short or Long Rest)
actions: Mass Telekinesis (Gem Awakening Only; Recharges after a Short or Long Rest)

name: Empyrean
lore: Empyreans are the celestial children of the gods of the Upper Planes. They are universally beautiful, statuesque, and self-assured.

name: Encephalon Cluster
sourceFull: Phandelver and Below: The Shattered Obelisk
lore: An encephalon cluster pays no heed to an elder brain or its mind flayer progenitors. This grotesque creature shambles through dark and forgotten caverns, spewing warped progeny so it can multiply.
actions: Spawn Progeny (Recharges after a Short or Long Rest)

name: Encephalon Gemmule
sourceFull: Phandelver and Below: The Shattered Obelisk
lore: Due to the influence of the Far Realm, the eggs within an encephalon cluster no longer hatch into illithid tadpoles. Instead, when one of an encephalon cluster's eggs reaches maturity, it is ejected from the mass as an encephalon gemmule.

name: Enchanter
lore: Enchanters are specialist wizards who understand how to alter and control minds using magic. They might be personable and interesting, using magic to manipulate people only when banter and conventional persuasion fails, or they might be rude and demanding, using and relying on {@condition charmed}, obedient minions.

name: Enchanter Wizard
sourceFull: Mordenkainen Presents: Monsters of the Multiverse

name: Enchanting Infiltrator
lore: An antagonist for tier 2 play (levels 5 to 10), the enchanting infiltrator is a Fey that beguiles and misdirects. This villain resides in a place of power and safety, whether that's a hidden thieves' guildhall or a lavish manse.

name: Endelyn Moongrave
lore: Endelyn, also known by the names Creeping Lyn and Bitter End, is the youngest member of the Hourglass Coven. People come to her to learn about their futures, which she spells out through grotesque theatrical pageants.

name: Ender Dragon
lore: The mighty Ender Dragon is one of a kind—a vast, flying creature with void-black scales and purple eyes. It soars above the central island of the End.

name: Enderman
lore: Endermen are tall, black, bipedal creatures with long, thin limbs and piercing, purple eyes. Violet particles flicker in and out of existence around them.

name: Envy
lore: The iron lion, Envy, is one of two guardians that Zybilna created to watch over her garden in her absence. When Zybilna became frozen in time, her guardians appeared from their respective demiplanes to perform their duty.

name: Eo Ashmajiir
lore: Born from the experiments of Belic Haphrat, a Red Wizard from the lands of Thay, Eo is a powerful tiefling sorceress. The offspring of a half-dragon and a succubus, Eo was raised by Belic as his own child, and showed an early affinity for magical gifts.

name: Equinal Guardinal
lore: The horselike equinals are among the strongest of the guardinals. Equinals have long faces and crested manes, and their powerful, hocked legs carry them over great distances.

name: Erky Timbers
lore: Acolytes are junior members of a clergy, usually answerable to a priest. They perform a variety of functions in a temple and are granted minor spellcasting power by their deities.

name: Ervan Soulfallen
actions: Acid Rain (Recharges after a Short or Long Rest)

name: Esthetic
lore: An esthetic is a biological, symbiotic creation of a reigar. It is essentially an organic ship with only the barest hint of awareness.

name: Eternal Flame Guardian
lore: Eternal Flame guardians are dullards fascinated by the power of fire and eager to show the cult's enemies firsthand what it feels like to burn. They light things aflame to honor elemental fire and just for entertainment, but they don't always think ahead about what else nearby might catch fire.
traits: Flaming Weapon (Recharges after a Short or Long Rest)

name: Eternal Flame Priest
lore: Eternal Flame priests see the world around them as impure and unworthy, and believe that only cleansing by fire can set it right. As a result, all fire is deemed holy, from the smallest candle flame to the greatest conflagrations.

name: Ettercap
lore: Ettercaps are humanoid spiders that tend, feed, and watch over spiders the way a shepherd oversees a flock of sheep. They lair deep in remote forests.

name: Ettin
lore: An ettin is a foul, two-headed giant with the crude characteristics of an orc. It never bathes if it can help it, and its thick skin is usually encrusted with a thick layer of dirt and grime beneath the stinking hides it wears.

name: Ettin Ceremorph
sourceFull: Bigby Presents: Glory of the Giants
lore: Mind flayers, which are described in the {@i Monster Manual}, are created through ceremorphosis, a process that begins with the implantation of an illithid tadpole in the brain of a Humanoid host. Mind flayers have subjected giants to this process in an effort to create larger, stronger mind flayers, but those experiments all ended in failure: a giant's body is simply too large for a single tadpole to take over.

name: Euryale
lore: When she retired from adventuring, Euryale relocated to Cair Ophidian, a small hideaway in the Outlands detailed later in chapter 22. There, Euryale embraced the quiet life of a gardener, and she spends her time tending to the plants she's cultivated around her home.

name: Evil Mage
lore: Evil mages hunger for arcane power and dwell in isolated places, where they can perform terrible magical experiments without interference.

name: Evoker
lore: Evokers are specialist wizards who harness magical energy and elemental forces to destroy. Many tend to be hotheaded and aggressive.

name: Evoker Wizard
sourceFull: Mordenkainen Presents: Monsters of the Multiverse

name: Expeditious Messenger
sourceFull: Eberron: Rising from the Last War
languages: one language spoken by its creator

name: Exul
lore: With his skin aglow like searing coal, a beard like sheafs of burnt paper, and hair billowing like smoke, Exul's physical form does nothing to hide his elemental nature. He was born as a spirit of earth and flame.

name: Eye Monger
lore: When its large eye and mouth are closed, an eye monger looks like nothing more than a 12-foot-diameter asteroid. When it senses vibrations in the space around it, the eye monger opens its eye and reveals its true, menacing nature.

name: Eye of Fear and Flame
lore: An eye of fear and flame stalks the world of the living, commanding individuals to perform evil deeds of the undead's choosing or be destroyed. If its commands are not obeyed, an eye of fear and flame throws back its hood to reveal its skull—set with gems of red and black in each eye socket—then attacks.
languages: all languages known by any creature within 30 feet of it

name: Eyedrake
lore: When a beholder contends with a dragon for power and wealth, its thoughts of its dragon rival can become deeply obsessive, eventually pervading the beholder's dreams. If the rivalry lasts long enough, these fevered dreams can manifest as an eyedrake—a beholder-like creature with draconic features.

name: Ezmerelda d'Avenir
lore: Ezmerelda d'Avenir, a Vistana, is the protégé of Rudolph van Richten - despite the fact that her first encounter with the vampire hunter was anything but pleasant. When Ezmerelda was a little girl, her family kidnapped van Richten's teenage son, Erasmus, and delivered him into the clutches of a vampire.
actions: Evil Eye (Recharges after a Short or Long Rest)

name: Factol Skall
lore: Skall is the current factol of the Heralds of Dust and the only leader the faction has ever had. A popular Duster legend holds that other than the Lady of Pain herself, Skall is Sigil's oldest resident, the first creature to live and die in the City of Doors.

name: Faerl
lore: Faerl, a neutral evil wood elf, is proud and cruel. He believes that he must be in control at all times.

name: Falcon the Hunter
lore: Adventurers encounter Falcon the Hunter if they visit his hunting lodge in Neverwinter Wood.

name: Faldorn
lore: Faldorn is a human druid who is secretly a Shadow Master, one of the leaders of a militant sect of druids called the Shadow Druids. She is a devout follower of the beliefs of the Shadow Druids, believing that civilization is a cancer that threatens to make all the mortal races into weaklings.

name: False Lich
lore: Occasionally, liches create nefarious magical copies of themselves to fool enemies, to guard treasure, or for other inscrutable reasons. To create a false lich, a lich binds a shred of its life force to a corpse in a profane ritual.

name: Farastu Demodand
lore: Farastus, also known as tarry demodands, are the least of the demodands. These violent Fiends ooze thick, sticky tar that sticks to anything it touches.

name: Fastieth
sourceFull: Eberron: Rising from the Last War

name: Fate Hag
lore: Fate hags are servants of destiny that mostly reside in the Feywild and Shadowfell or near crossings to those realms from the Material Plane. The aspects of fate to which a fate hag is most attuned depends on the plane where the hag dwells—hopeful and prosperous in the Feywild or dire and doomed in the Shadowfell.

name: Fated Shaker
lore: Fated shakers are bullies who shake down those indebted to the faction's tax collectors and evictors. These enforcers use their magic to intimidate and subjugate those who try to stand up to them.

name: Fathomer
lore: Fathomers are cultists who have sealed a pact with Olhydra, Princess of Evil Water. In addition to their spellcasting abilities, fathomers have the ability to transform their bodies into water, taking serpent-like shapes.

name: Feathergale Knight
lore: The Feathergale Knights are ambitious, wealthy folk from cities such as Waterdeep seduced by the air cult's promises of power. From the backs of hippogriffs or giant vultures, they survey the land around Feathergale Spire, guarding the entrance to the air cult's hidden temple.

name: Felidar
lore: A felidar is a celestial creature whose nature reflects an inherent devotion to law and order. It resembles an enormous cat with two pairs of downward-sloping horns and prominent teeth.
languages: understands Celestial and Common but can't speak

name: Female Steeder
sourceFull: Mordenkainen Presents: Monsters of the Multiverse

name: Fensir Devourer
sourceFull: Bigby Presents: Glory of the Giants

name: Fensir Skirmisher
sourceFull: Bigby Presents: Glory of the Giants

name: Feral Ashenwight
sourceFull: Phandelver and Below: The Shattered Obelisk
languages: understands the languages it knew in life but can't speak

name: Ferrumach Rilmani
lore: Ferrumachs are the foot soldiers of the rilmani, preserving the balance between the planes through martial force. They vigilantly defend the Spire and fight at the command of aurumachs to protect the neutrality of the multiverse.

name: Fewmaster Gholcag
sourceFull: Dragonlance: Shadow of the Dragon Queen
lore: Fewmaster Gholcag is a low-ranking commander in the Red Dragon Army and the leader of the raid on Vogler. Ogres are hulking giants notorious for their quick tempers.

name: Feyr
lore: A feyr (pronounced "fear") is a tentacled horror that feeds quietly on strong emotions. To minimize the chance of harm to itself, it prefers to devour the nightmares of other creatures while they sleep.

name: Fiendish Auger
sourceFull: Phandelver and Below: The Shattered Obelisk
lore: A fiendish auger is created when a wicked spirit enters an excavation drill, causing the hulking corkscrew bore to glow brightly with hellfire. Fueled by the fervent aggression of the evil spirit within, fiendish augers can rapidly churn through solid rock.

name: Fiendish Flesh Golem
lore: A fiendish flesh golem is a particularly big flesh golem made of stitched-together body parts from devils, demons, yugoloths, and other fiends. As these parts can come from different creatures and be combined in different ways, no two fiendish flesh golems look exactly alike.
languages: understands the languages of its creator but can't speak

name: Fiendish Icon
lore: {@i Effigy Animated by Hellish Magic} Fiendish icons are grotesque, sapient statues depicting fearsome fiends. These creatures gain life as a result of sinister rituals.
languages: understands Common and Infernal but can't speak

name: Fierna
lore: Fierna rules Phlegethos alongside her father, Belial. She is emotionally intelligent, and her charisma has earned her many allies and few enemies in the Nine Hells.

name: Firbolg Primeval Warden
sourceFull: Bigby Presents: Glory of the Giants

name: Firbolg Wanderer
sourceFull: Bigby Presents: Glory of the Giants

name: Fire Elemental Myrmidon
sourceFull: Mordenkainen Presents: Monsters of the Multiverse
languages: one language of its creator's choice

name: Fire Giant Dreadnought
sourceFull: Mordenkainen Presents: Monsters of the Multiverse
lore: When I first saw a fire giant dreadnought, I doubted the giant could even move. I quickly learned my error.

name: Fire Giant Forgecaller
sourceFull: Bigby Presents: Glory of the Giants
lore: A fire giant who masters traditional rune magic can control the heat of a volcanic environment and conjure raw elemental energy of fire and magma. In a fire giant community, these forgecallers oversee both forges and the community's defense, often in Surtur's name.

name: Fire Giant of Evil Fire
sourceFull: Bigby Presents: Glory of the Giants
lore: A fire giant is born around flame, works with flame, and lives among flames, but a fire giant who gazes into flames too deeply may see the Prince of Evil Fire, Imix, gazing back. Giants who fall to Imix's corruption might leave their homes to join with like-minded cultists, but others remain within their communities to pursue Imix's destructive agenda.

name: Fire Hellion
sourceFull: Bigby Presents: Glory of the Giants
lore: To my mind, the fire hellion perfectly illustrates the difference between natural fire and the corrupt flames of the Nine Hells. Fire giants study war in its many forms across the multiverse, which sometimes leads the giants to a fascination with the Blood War—the endless conflict between demons and devils across the Lower Planes.

name: Firefist
lore: Boros firefists combine potent magic with peerless fighting ability, inspiring all who serve alongside them. They often act as the point of contact between the Boros Legion and the angelic leaders.

name: Firegaunt
sourceFull: Bigby Presents: Glory of the Giants
lore: A fire giant whose burning hatred prevents it from moving on to the afterlife rises as a firegaunt. Spawned from deep rancor, the firegaunt seeks out living creatures to destroy.

name: Firemane Angel
traits: Relentless (Recharges after a Short or Long Rest)

name: Firenewt Warlock of Imix
sourceFull: Mordenkainen Presents: Monsters of the Multiverse

name: Firenewt Warrior
sourceFull: Mordenkainen Presents: Monsters of the Multiverse
actions: Spit Fire (Recharges after a Short or Long Rest)

name: First-Year Student
lore: A student who has not yet chosen one of Strixhaven's five mystic colleges is referred to as a first-year student, after the one year of basic curriculum that most students must complete before selecting their school. These beginner students are taught by faculty from all of Strixhaven's colleges, learning rudimentary magic as they acclimate to the university's environment.

name: Fist of Bane
